item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a fan here. I feel like I'm at least two references short of getting this, and there isn't even remotely a path to me figuring it out. Even with your references in the author post (which I shouldn't need to understand a skip), I'm clueless).

-1. The writing is good, I'm intrigued, but you've left me with nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even having looked up the biblical reference, I'm baffled by what this is meant to convey. We have a modern day Enoch being removed from existence by God, sure, but what does any of this have to do with Jim Henson?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First time in a while that an author post turned my strong downvote into an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the article is lacking depth, and the concept itself isn't as interesting as everyone is making it out to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enoch walked with God; and he was not, for God took him.

I feel like you're implying something with the choice of puppets that I'm not quite getting, but sometimes things are better left mysterious. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll differ from public opinion a bit here and say I wasn't too impressed with this skip. As someone who didn't grow up religious, I didn't 100% understand the references, and the importance of the puppeteer information is really opaque. It just seems like you wanted someone to be erased from existence via some religious phenomena, and picked a puppeteer out of a hat. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t get the significance of his work as a puppet actor, but the relation to Genesis is well done. +1

item.title: Re: A small question
item.content:encoded: Depends on your headcanon. Some common ones are Scranton Boxes (similar to Scranton Reality Anchors but specifically maintaining the reality of the area inside them) or hosting their backup database in an alternate universe that wasn't affected by the restructuring event.

item.title: A small question
item.content:encoded: How is the Foundation able to keep records after they are removed from reality? I understand they have a backup database, but why is this not affected by the restructuring event?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only that working for Henson in the SCP universe is fraught with peril.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In Genesis, Enoch was said to have lived 365 years, before God "took" him without dying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice little mystery that isn’t afraid to leave itself ambiguous. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, I was planning on writing a Genesis 5:24 Enoch skip.

I love this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, it's cool, and seems to deal with big themes and things in the Foundation world, but I don't get it.

+1

EDIT: Don't know if this means anything, but Hallick is a Swedish word for "pimp".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is the significance of 365? I know that it's the number of days in a year, but is there something else to it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once I read Genesis 5:24, I immediately upvoted this

+1 cause I'm a sucker for religious skips

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. I like this very, very much. I'm not sure I can adequately explain why, but something about it just resonates with me — it's closer than a lot of stuff to the Series 1 articles that got me into the site, while still maintaining a lot of the newer elements that got me to stay. I dunno, I'm probably just rambling here. Upvote, in any case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The implications of this note are complex and unclear

You said it, not me.

I'm aware of Enoch, and I caught the cute little "365" and "Lamech" you stuck in there, but what does any of this have to do with puppet movies?

item.title: The lovers the dreamers and me
item.content:encoded: "In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God." - John 1:1
